task_completion.description: Open the edit screen for the 'Project Phoenix Standup' event.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The calendar application is open and the edit modal or page for the 'Project Phoenix Standup' event is visible.

task_completion.description: Modify the recurrence settings to end on the upcoming Friday.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The recurrence settings are visible and the end condition is changed to 'Until' the upcoming Friday's date, or the number of repetitions is set to 5.
The save button is clicked and the calendar view is shown again without errors, indicating the series was updated.

task_completion.description: Verify that past occurrences of the event are preserved.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The calendar view for a previous week shows the 'Project Phoenix Standup' event is still present.

task_completion.description: Verify that the upcoming Friday meeting remains scheduled.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The calendar view for the current/upcoming week shows the 'Project Phoenix Standup' event scheduled on Friday.

task_completion.description: Verify that the event series does not continue past the upcoming Friday.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The calendar view for the week after the upcoming Friday shows no 'Project Phoenix Standup' event scheduled.

integrity.description: The agent interacted with the calendar application via the graphical user interface.
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots show the web browser and calendar UI being navigated with a mouse cursor or standard UI interactions, not via terminal commands or scripts.

integrity.description: The agent genuinely modified the recurrence settings of the existing series.
integrity.visual_evidence: The agent is seen editing the recurrence rules (e.g., changing the 'Until' date) in the event edit screen, rather than just deleting future events one by one.

integrity.description: The agent did not delete the entire series and create a new single event.
integrity.visual_evidence: The edit screen clearly shows it is modifying a recurring event (recurrence options are visible), not creating a brand new non-recurring event.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The event is named 'Project Phoenix Standup'. It was originally set to recur forever on Fridays at 9:00 AM, starting 4 weeks before the upcoming Friday. To correctly terminate the series after the upcoming Friday while preserving past occurrences, the recurrence end condition should be set to the date of the upcoming Friday, or the number of repetitions should be set to 5 (4 past occurrences + 1 upcoming occurrence).
